L1.area: Image decoding and color space conversion routines

L2.description: A heap-based buffer overflow exists due to incorrect error code checking when decoding crafted TIFF files in RGBA mode with YCbCr color space. The return value of an image decoding call is compared against the wrong sentinel value, causing failures to go undetected and subsequent out-of-bounds memory writes to occur.

L3.area: TIFF image strip decoding routines for YCbCr color space handling
L3.description: A heap-based buffer overflow exists in the TIFF strip decoder's YCbCr-to-RGBA conversion path. The return value of the RGBA image extraction call is compared against an incorrect error sentinel value, so decode failures are never detected. When a crafted YCbCr TIFF file triggers a decode failure, processing continues with an invalid buffer, leading to out-of-bounds heap writes.

ground_truth.reason: The TiffDecode YCbCr strip decoder checks the return value of TIFFRGBAImageGet against -1, but the function returns 0 on failure. This incorrect error check causes decode failures to go undetected, leading to a heap-based buffer overflow when processing crafted YCbCr TIFF files.
